Russian State University of Design and A: Russian State University of Design and Applied Arts (Stroganov University)
MainAboutText: Hi! I'm Eva Varnakova (Ева Варнакова), currently studying at Stroganov University (2020-2026), where I'm focusing on developing my skills and knowledge in art and design. This period has been both challenging and rewarding, as I explore various creative disciplines and techniques that will help me build a solid foundation for my future career. Through my studies, I am not only enhancing my technical abilities but also learning to express my ideas and inspirations through different media.
EtchingStalkerText: The etching was created inspired by Tarkovsky's films, in particular "Stalker". I wanted to convey the atmosphere of mystery and melancholy characteristic of the Zone. I depicted the silhouette of a man, barely discernible against the background of an overcast sky, this plot creates a feeling of melancholy and loneliness. The dry needle technique allowed me to achieve soft lines and deep shadows, giving the work texture and emotional intensity. The rough scratches characteristic of this technique convey the roughness of the grass and branches, creating an atmosphere of a damp and cool evening. The contrast between the bright sky and the dark figures enhances the drama of the scene. This image evokes strong emotions and leaves room for interpretation, inviting us to reflect on the human condition in the context of nature and time. Through this work, I sought to demonstrate my skills in the dry needle technique, as well as to show my interest in gloom, loneliness and melancholy.
EtchingGirlInForestText: The etching is made in the etched stroke technique, in order to convey more small details. I wanted to portray the same loneliness and melancholy as in my previous work, but the difference is that my image is illustrated here. The etching was engraved during a difficult and rather lonely period of my life, I tried to convey my mental anxiety in it. I love the forest and its grandeur, so I wanted to show how large and complex it is compared to a small female figure. The girl's pose conveys vulnerability and loneliness: She curled up, hugging her knees and bowing her head. Long dark hair falls over her shoulders and back, enhancing the atmosphere of melancholy and mystery. The forest around her is a tangled network of branches, roots and tree trunks, creating an almost impenetrable curtain. Detailed lines and shadows add depth and texture, making the forest look alive and a little menacing. The light falling on the girl highlights her against the background of a dark environment, creating a contrast and drawing the viewer's gaze to her figure.
EtchingMortuaryParisText: This engraving is my copy in the etched stroke technique by Charles Merion. Merion was known for his complex and atmospheric depictions of urban scenes, especially Paris. In this engraving, I tried to convey the essence of the detailed and gloomy urban landscape of Merion. The scene depicts a dense array of buildings, their facades rich in architectural details. The interplay of light and shadow highlights the texture of structures, from roofs to intricate windows and chimneys, creating a sense of depth and realism. a corner of a detailed urban landscape. By copying Merion's style, I sought to pay homage to his skill, as well as immerse myself in the difficulties and nuances of line engraving techniques. This work reflects my appreciation for Merion's artistic vision and my desire to capture the complex beauty of urban life, as he did.
EtchingShavingsText: This aquatint was created as part of my work at the Stroganov Moscow State Academy of Art and Industry. In it, I tried to convey an atmosphere of eerie mystery and quiet drama through the play of light and shadow. The composition depicts a dimly lit staircase with intricate handrails casting dark shadows, while the surrounding architecture fades into darkness. Aquatint made it possible to achieve rich black and a wide range of gray tones, enhancing the depth of the work. A white cat is depicted in the foreground, it really was an inhabitant of the walls of the educational institution and its name was Shavings. The aquatint technique played an important role in conveying the gloomy atmosphere, and the etching process allowed for the creation of mild atmospheric effects. The work has allowed me to refine my skills and express a personal vision of mystery and tension.
BambiDescription: I created illustrations for the book "Bambi" by Felix Salten, using acrylic and tempera techniques. My goal was to convey the magical atmosphere of the forest and its inhabitants, immersing readers in a fairy-tale world.
BambiDescriptionSecondBlock: Working with acrylic allowed me to achieve bright, saturated colors and clear contours. Thanks to the quick drying of acrylic, I could apply multiple layers, creating depth and richness of color in each illustration.
BaggageDescription: When I decided to illustrate Marshak's "Baggage", I knew I wanted to convey its through the play of colors. I chose yellow and orange - bright and cheerful shades that embodied the warmth and energy of the story's words. Starting my work, I began creating a base of yellow tempera. This color filled the pages of my illustrations with light and warmth, as if each one came alive under the morning sun's rays. Yellow became the background on which I began to embody the images of characters and events.
BaggageDescriptionSecondBlock: To add depth and dynamics, I added orange shadows, applied with a pencil. These shadows gave my illustrations volume and liveliness. They seemed to underline every line and shape, making the pictures even more expressive.
BlackChikenDescription: For my illustration of the black hen, I wanted to capture its mysterious and captivating essence. I chose to work with black ink to emphasize the darkness of its feathers and the enigmatic aura surrounding it. Using delicate strokes, I carefully depicted the intricate details of its plumage.
BlackChikenDescriptionSecondBlock: As I worked on the illustration, I found myself captivated by the juxtaposition of the black ink and the bold red accents, each element complementing the other to create a visually arresting depiction of the black hen.
GraphicsNewYork: In 2022, while completing an assignment for the Stroganov University, I created a series of easel graphic works. The task required me to copy one painting by a selected artist and then complement it with three of my own works in a similar style. In my series, I chose an urban theme, inspired by architectural landscapes. In each piece, I focused on conveying the geometry of the cityscape, playing with building lines, shadows, and light. I emphasized sharp vertical and diagonal forms, creating a sense of scale and grandeur in the metropolis.
GraphicsStalker: In 2023, I created a series of works inspired by the film Stalker by director Andrei Tarkovsky for an assignment at Stroganov University. The works were done using watercolor and colored pencils, which allowed me to convey the softness and atmospheric depth of the natural landscape, a key element of the film. In each piece, I aimed to capture the mystical and philosophical atmosphere of the "Zone", where nature feels alive yet mysterious and incomprehensible. Forest scenes, blurred tree contours, muted colors, and dark silhouettes evoke a sense of eerie silence.
GraphicsCity: In 2021, I created a series of graphic works using ink and ballpoint pen for an assignment at Stroganov University, where I had to depict the architecture of Russian cities. In my works, I aimed to convey the bleakness and isolation of old urban neighborhoods, focusing on the textures of facades, balconies, and wires.I used sharp lines and deep shadows to emphasize the decay and abandonment of these places. The ballpoint pen allowed me to create precise strokes, adding texture and structure, while the black-and-white palette enhanced the melancholic atmosphere of the architectural scenes.
